stem: A rectangular field is 16 km by 12 km. Fences run along all four sides and along both diagonals, which cross at the centre (each diagonal is 20 km long). A farmer starts at one corner, walks along every fence at least once, and returns to that same corner. What is the shortest total distance, in kilometres, she can walk?
method_star: Count how many fences meet at each junction: if any has an ODD number you cannot do it without repeats. Pair up the odd junctions and re-walk only the cheapest connecting fences; answer = total fence length + that minimum extra.
trap: just adding up all the fences and forgetting you must double back on the odd junctions.
